trustBrief.userJob: Understand how Sanchika turns source, uncertainty, and human review into inspectable interface contracts.
trustBrief.primaryDecision: Choose a qualifying identity direction that can carry the production Sanchika gallery.
trustBoundaries: Sanchika supplies contracts and a review loop, not compliance judgment, customer evidence, or an application runtime.
AX-031 is synthetic evidence and every review result is an AI proxy, not user validation.
evidenceRequirements: Source, uncertainty, review state, owner accountability, and validation limits remain distinct and visible.

claims.evidence: Repository-owned AX-031 fixture, browser captures, calibrated isolated reviews, and recognition proxies

nonGoals: No AI runtime, automated compliance judgment, customer proof, authority signal, dashboard, provider adapter, or raster dependency.

trustBrief.userJob: Understand how Sanchika turns source, uncertainty, and human review into inspectable interface contracts.

designBrief.firstViewportSignal: Make regulated interfaces that show their work.
designBrief.emotionalIntent: Prepared, clear, accountable, and unmistakably crafted without borrowing authority.
narrativeArc: State the promise and contract boundary
responsiveConstraints: Mobile preserves source, interpretation, and review in semantic reading order without overflow.
Desktop uses code-native plane geometry while keeping all three accountable parts legible.
verificationEvidence: Browser and accessibility verification before production
nonGoals: No dashboard shell, fake authority, generic AI SaaS language, rasterized UI, decorative motion, or motion-gated content.

disqualificationReasons: Only three of four reviewers preferred it to the current baseline.
The initial accessibility veto was not the territory's only material craft concern.

iterations.changeHypothesis: Raising secondary-text contrast and size and allowing resilient wrapping will clear the accessibility veto without changing territory identity.

vetoes: direction-gamma does not preserve co-equal accountable parts
direction-gamma remains too dominant and crowded as a default family grammar

renderEvidence.finding: Two anonymized code-native directions qualified for owner selection.
Honest same-commit plain-request control retained for comparison only; its code was discarded.
The only shared legibility veto cleared after a 4-of-4 preferred revision and a median increase from 1 to 4.

adoptionEvidence.verificationRun: Gated lab build, gallery typecheck, repository validation, calibrated review, and 3-of-3 recognition completed.
adoptionEvidence.rollbackPlan: Keep the lab excluded from normal builds and delete rejected direction code until owner approval.

residualRisks: Production browser, accessibility, and performance gates have not started.
All review and recognition results are AI proxy evidence, not user validation.
nextActions: Owner selects Option Alpha, Option Beta, or rejects both.
Advance only an approved qualifying direction to the production build phase.

nextAction: Await C2R-A owner selection between anonymized Option Alpha and Option Beta.
